title: Warm Spicy Middle Eastern Lamb Kebabs
description: A crowd-pleasing Middle Eastern dish featuring lamb kebabs marinated in a mixture of spices and kombu, served warm and spicy, this recipe is a must try for any lamb lover, with a unique blend of flavors that will leave you wanting more.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the crushed kombu to the marinade and stir to combine.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each cube.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they are cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve the kebabs warm, garnished with chopped fresh parsley and a side of tzatziki sauce if desired.
